entities.name: IRRIGATION FREQUENCY
entities.description: The frequency at which irrigation is applied to crops.

entities.name: SOIL NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of managing nutrients in the soil to optimize crop growth.

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER IRRIGATION
entities.description: Irrigation systems managed by small-scale farmers.

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: The country where the study on smallholder irrigation was conducted.

entities.name: CHAMELEON
entities.description: A monitoring tool used to facilitate decision-making and learning about soilwater-nutrient dynamics.

entities.name: WETTING FRONT DETECTOR
entities.description: A monitoring tool used to facilitate decision-making and learning about soilwater-nutrient dynamics.

entities.name: SOILWATER-NUTRIENT DYNAMICS
entities.description: The interaction and movement of water and nutrients within the soil.

entities.name: NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential elements required by plants for growth, managed within the root zone.

entities.name: ROOT ZONE
entities.description: The area of soil surrounding plant roots where water and nutrients are absorbed.

entities.name: NUMBER OF SIPHONS
entities.description: The quantity of siphons used during irrigation events.

entities.name: EVENT DURATION
entities.description: The length of time for a single irrigation event.

entities.name: WATER PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop yield per unit of water used.

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Technical tools and methods used to improve irrigation systems.

entities.name: FARMERS
entities.description: Small-scale agricultural producers managing irrigation systems.

entities.name: LEARNING ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: A setting that fosters critical feedback mechanisms and knowledge acquisition.

entities.name: MARKET CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Economic limitations that act as feedback mechanisms in a learning environment.

relationships.description: Interacts to influence the sustainability of irrigation systems
Reducing frequency helps retain nutrients in the root zone
Reducing the number of siphons helps retain nutrients in the root zone
Reducing event duration helps retain nutrients in the root zone
